g.text: “Before my full awakening, when I was still an unawakened bodhisatta,
it occurred to me: Suppose that I divide my thoughts into two classes.”
TWO CLASSES OF VITAKKA · dvidhā vitakkā
Both classes have their own appropriate response
reflect on drawbacks · when seen, the thought subsides
Obstructs wisdom · afflictive · not conducive to Nibbāna
Whenever it arises again, immediately abandoned
recognize harmlessness · safe to ponder, yet not endlessly
But over-pondering tires the body and agitates the mind
kāyo kilameyya · ūhate citte · ārā samādhimhā
So that the mind might not become agitated
“Whatever one frequently thinks upon and ponders, that becomes the inclination of the mind.”
yaññadeva bahulamanuvitakketi anuvicāreti, tathā tathā nati hoti cetaso
Unrelenting energy · clear mindfulness · tranquil and unexcited body · collected and unified mind
āraddhaṁ viriyam · asammuṭṭhā sati · passaddho kāyo asāraddho · samāhitaṁ cittaṁ ekaggaṁ
Born of seclusion (vivekajaṁ) · Imbued with uplifting joy and pleasure (pītisukhaṁ)
Internal tranquility (ajjhattaṁ sampasādanaṁ) · Unification of mind (ekodibhāvaṁ)
Free from reflection and examination (avitakkaṁ · avicāraṁ)
Born of collectedness (samādhijaṁ) · Imbued with uplifting joy and pleasure (pītisukhaṁ)
With the fading of desire for uplifting joy (pītiyā ca virāgā)
Experiencing pleasure with the body (sukhaṁ ca kāyena paṭisaṁvedeti)
Abandoning bodily pleasure and pain (sukhassa ca pahānā dukkhassa ca pahānā)
Settling of mental pleasure and displeasure (somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā)
With mind collected, purified, clarified, blemish-free, flexible, stable, and unshakeable
samāhite citte parisuddhe pariyodāte anaṅgaṇe vigatūpakkilese mudubhūte kammaniye ṭhite āneñjappatte
Simile · traveler recollecting villages passed by
Sees beings passing away and reappearing
Simile · seeing comings and goings between two houses
its ending, and the way of practice
Simile · clear undisturbed mountain lake — contents visible
“Birth is ended; the spiritual life has been lived; what had to be done has been done.”
khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā
THE MARSH AND THE HERD · DEER SIMILE
seeking ruin, harm, and bondage of beings
seeking good, welfare, and safety of beings
“The safe path reopened; the wrong path closed; decoy removed; dummy destroyed.”
The great marsh is sensual pleasures · the herd are beings · the harmful man is Māra · the caring man is the Tathāgata
the false path is the wrong eightfold path · the safe path is the Noble Eightfold Path
“Here are the roots of trees, here are the empty huts. Meditate, do not be negligent!”
